summary: Loader package requires undeclared scoped dependency at import time
details: @node-cloud/create's main module (index.js) begins with `var ins = require('@sqlite-node/createsql');` — an unconditional top-level require of a scoped package that is not listed in package.json's dependencies. The declared dependency list instead contains a differently-named suspicious scoped package (`@sql-trigger/nodesql`). On `require('@node-cloud/create')`, Node will resolve and execute `@sqlite-node/createsql`, pulling whatever code is registered under that scope name into the consumer's process. The remainder of index.js is a copy of a generic npm-publish helper (`publish`, `shouldPublish`, `npmAddUser`, reads of NPM_USERNAME/NPM_PASSWORD/NPM_EMAIL) which has no relationship to the README's stated purpose ("Cloud data for JavaScript") or to the package name. The combination of (a) name resembling a namespace squat against `@node-cloud`, (b) doc/code mismatch where the body is unrelated boilerplate, and (c) an undeclared external scoped require at module top is the launcher/loader shape used to silently pull attacker-controlled scoped code into installers' dependency trees.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
